title: Local businesses connect at networking evening
description: GEORGE NEWS - The George Business Chamber...
bodyText:
Gallery

GEORGE NEWS - The George Business Chamber hosted a networking evening with an amazing view at the Hilltop Boutique Hotel in Victoria Bay on Thursday evening, 30 October.


> These networking evenings typically bring together local business owners and professionals to connect, collaborate and get to know one another.


Members exchange business cards, introduce their services and explore opportunities for collaboration over light snacks and refreshments.

The evenings offer the ideal setting to promote your own business and for you to connect with like-minded people from the area.

At Thursday night's event, Tony Wilson and his team of MDS Col-livery in George walked away with the coveted service excellence award, which recognised their outstanding commitment to customer service and reliability in the local business community.

The Hilltop Boutique Hotel is tucked away in a pristine fynbos nature reserve between Victoria Bay and Wilderness.

Set on 6ha of private land, this elegant retreat combines modern comfort with the beauty of its natural surroundings.

Guests can choose from stylish double rooms, a deluxe suite or a self-catering apartment - all with breathtaking sea and mountain views.

Wilbur van Schalkwyk (left) and Jody Wilson (third) of MDS Collivery accepted the service excellence award presented by Johan Lochner of PSG George and the George Business Chamber’s Irene Koegelenberg.Nelson Mandela University’s Milisa Piko, George Herald’s sales executive, Ilana van der Merwe, and Udy Obi of the Garden Route Innovation and Technology Hub. Rochelle Skead of Conscious Movement and Lisa de Speville of Lisa de Speville Mediation at the networking evening on Thursday.‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
